1	package agent
2	
3	// dispatch.go owns the SD-024 dispatch seam shared by execute-bead and the
4	// post-merge reviewer. It threads durable knobs from a sealed
5	// config.ResolvedConfig with per-invocation plumbing/intent from an
6	// AgentRunRuntime through the same service-or-runner routing used by
7	// RunWithConfigViaService for `ddx agent run`.
8	
9	import (
10		"context"
11		"fmt"
12	
13		agentlib "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent"
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
15	)
16	
17	// dispatchViaResolvedConfig is the internal SD-024 dispatch seam shared by
18	// execute-bead's worker and the post-merge reviewer. It resolves how the
19	// agent invocation is executed (test-injected runner, pre-built service, or
20	// a fresh service constructed from projectRoot) and routes through
21	// executeOnService so every durable knob lands on the dispatched request
22	// exactly once.
23	//
24	// Resolution order:
25	//  1. runner (test injection seam) — used directly via runner.Run after
26	//     applying any AgentRunRuntime overrides.
27	//  2. svc (pre-built service) — used via executeOnService.
28	//  3. Fallback: construct a fresh service via NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
29	//     and dispatch via executeOnService.
30	//
31	// Override fields on runtime (HarnessOverride, ModelOverride,
32	// PermissionsOverride, SessionLogDirOverride) take precedence over the
33	// matching rcfg accessors when non-empty, so callers can pin one knob for
34	// a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
35	func dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, svc agentlib.DdxAgent, runner AgentRunner, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
36		if runner != nil {
37			return runner.Run(buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx, rcfg, runtime))
38		}
39		if svc == nil {
40			built, err := NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
41			if err != nil {
42				return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: build service: %w", err)
43			}
44			svc = built
45		}
46		return executeOnService(ctx, svc, projectRoot, rcfg, runtime)
47	}
48	
49	// buildRunArgsFromConfig assembles a RunArgs value for the test
50	// injection runner path. It applies AgentRunRuntime overrides so a caller
51	// (execute-bead worker, post-merge reviewer) can pin one durable knob for
52	// a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
53	func buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) RunArgs {
54		harness := runtime.HarnessOverride
55		if harness == "" {
56			harness = rcfg.Harness()
57		}
58		model := runtime.ModelOverride
59		if model == "" {
60			model = rcfg.Model()
61		}
62		permissions := runtime.PermissionsOverride
63		if permissions == "" {
64			permissions = rcfg.Permissions()
65		}
66		sessionLogDir := runtime.SessionLogDirOverride
67		if sessionLogDir == "" {
68			sessionLogDir = rcfg.SessionLogDir()
69		}
70	
71		var opts RunArgs
72		opts.Context = ctx
73		opts.Harness = harness
74		// evidence:allow-unbounded reason="caller is responsible for bounding the prompt before invoking dispatchViaResolvedConfig; downstream executeOnService hits readPromptFileBounded for PromptFile inputs"
75		opts.Prompt = runtime.Prompt
76		opts.PromptFile = runtime.PromptFile
77		opts.PromptSource = runtime.PromptSource
78		opts.Correlation = runtime.Correlation
79		opts.Model = model
80		opts.Provider = rcfg.Provider()
81		opts.ModelRef = rcfg.ModelRef()
82		opts.Effort = rcfg.Effort()
83		opts.Timeout = rcfg.Timeout()
84		opts.WallClock = rcfg.WallClock()
85		opts.WorkDir = runtime.WorkDir
86		opts.Permissions = permissions
87		opts.SessionLogDir = sessionLogDir
88		return opts
89	}

18	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
19	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
20	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
21	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
22	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
23		Log          io.Writer
24		EventSink    io.Writer
25		ProgressCh   chan<- ProgressEvent
26		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
27		Once         bool
28		PollInterval time.Duration
29		NoReview     bool
30		LabelFilter  string
31		SessionID    string
32		WorkerID     string
33		ProjectRoot  string
34	}
35	
36	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
37	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
38	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
39	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
40	
41	type ExecuteBeadReport struct {
42		BeadID      string `json:"bead_id"`
43		AttemptID   string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
44		WorkerID    string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
45		Harness     string `json:"harness,omitempty"`
46		Provider    string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
47		Model       string `json:"model,omitempty"`
48		Status      string `json:"status"`
49		Detail      string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
50		SessionID   string `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
51		BaseRev     string `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
52		ResultRev   string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
53		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
54		RetryAfter  string `json:"retry_after,omitempty"`
55		// NoChangesRationale carries the agent's explanation when status == no_changes.
56		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
57		// ReviewVerdict is the post-merge review verdict (APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES,
58		// or BLOCK) when a reviewer ran. Empty when review was skipped.
59		ReviewVerdict string `json:"review_verdict,omitempty"`
60		// ReviewRationale carries the actionable reviewer-authored findings for
61		// non-APPROVE review outcomes.
62		ReviewRationale string `json:"review_rationale,omitempty"`
63		// Tier is the model tier used for the final attempt (cheap, standard, smart).
64		// Populated by tier-escalating executors; empty for single-tier attempts.
65		Tier string `json:"tier,omitempty"`
66		// ProbeResult is a brief summary of the provider health probe at attempt time.
67		ProbeResult string `json:"probe_result,omitempty"`
68		// CostUSD is the dollar cost of this attempt as reported by the harness.

82		// sub-bead titles when Status == declined_needs_decomposition. The loop
83		// records these on the bead as a `decomposition-recommendation` event so

92	type ExecuteBeadExecutor interface {
93		Execute(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error)
94	}

97	
98	func (f ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc) Execute(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
99		return f(ctx, beadID)
100	}

102	// SatisfactionChecker evaluates whether a bead that returned no_changes is
103	// already satisfied and should be closed, or is still unresolved and should
104	// receive retry suppression. noChangesCount is the cumulative count including
105	// the current attempt.
106	//
107	// When satisfied is true the caller closes the bead with the returned evidence
108	// string as the detail. When false the caller applies a retry cooldown and
109	// leaves the bead open.
110	type SatisfactionChecker interface {
111		CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (satisfied bool, evidence string, err error)
112	}
113	
114	// SatisfactionCheckerFunc is a functional adapter for SatisfactionChecker.
115	type SatisfactionCheckerFunc func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (bool, string, error)
116	
117	func (f SatisfactionCheckerFunc) CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (bool, string, error) {

123		Claim(id, assignee string) error

125		Heartbeat(id string) error

127		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error

129		SetExecutionCooldown(id string, until time.Time, status, detail string) error
130		IncrNoChangesCount(id string) (int, error)
131		// Reopen sets a closed bead back to open, appending notes to the bead's
132		// Notes field and recording a reopen event. Used by the post-merge review
133		// step when the reviewer returns REQUEST_CHANGES or BLOCK.

137	// readyDiagnoser is the optional interface the work loop uses to explain an

144	// dependency-ready beads exist. Populated on an ExecuteBeadLoopResult when

147		SkippedEpics       []string `json:"skipped_epics,omitempty"`

154	// ProgressEvent is the FEAT-006 structured progress event. It is defined
155	// separately in the server package (server.ProgressEvent); this alias lets
156	// the agent package emit events without importing the server package.
157	// The field names and types are identical — the server package deserialises
158	// these directly from the channel.
159	//
160	// Terminal phases: done, preserved, failed.
161	type ProgressEvent struct {
162		EventID   string    `json:"event_id"`
163		AttemptID string    `json:"attempt_id"`
164		WorkerID  string    `json:"worker_id"`
165		ProjectID string    `json:"project_id"`

167		Harness   string    `json:"harness,omitempty"`
168		Model     string    `json:"model,omitempty"`
169		Profile   string    `json:"profile,omitempty"`
170		Phase     string    `json:"phase"`

172		Heartbeat bool      `json:"heartbeat"`

179		Attempts          int                  `json:"attempts"`
180		Successes         int                  `json:"successes"`
181		Failures          int                  `json:"failures"`
182		NoReadyWork       bool                 `json:"no_ready_work,omitempty"`
183		NoReadyWorkDetail NoReadyWorkBreakdown `json:"no_ready_work_detail,omitempty"`
184		LastSuccessAt     time.Time            `json:"last_success_at,omitempty"`
185		LastFailureStatus string               `json:"last_failure_status,omitempty"`
186		Results           []ExecuteBeadReport  `json:"results,omitempty"`
187	}

189	// ExecuteBeadWorker drains the current single-project execution-ready queue.
190	// It intentionally does not retry a failed/conflicted bead again in the same
191	// process run; a later operator-driven invocation can create the next attempt.
192	type ExecuteBeadWorker struct {
193		Store               ExecuteBeadLoopStore
194		Executor            ExecuteBeadExecutor
195		SatisfactionChecker SatisfactionChecker // nil → count-based default
196		Now                 func() time.Time
197		// Reviewer, when non-nil, is called after every successful merge to
198		// validate the commit against the bead's acceptance criteria. When nil,
199		// post-merge review is skipped (same behaviour as --no-review).
200		Reviewer BeadReviewer
201	}

203	// emitProgress sends a ProgressEvent to runtime.ProgressCh non-blocking.
204	// If ch is nil or full the event is silently dropped.
205	func emitProgress(ch chan<- ProgressEvent, evt ProgressEvent) {
206		if ch == nil {
207			return
208		}
209		select {
210		case ch <- evt:
211		default:
212		}
213	}
214	
215	// newProgressEvent builds a ProgressEvent with a random event_id and current timestamp.
216	func newProgressEvent(workerID, projectID, beadID, attemptID, harness, model, profile, phase string, phaseSeq int, heartbeat bool, elapsedMS int64) ProgressEvent {
217		return ProgressEvent{
218			EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
219			AttemptID: attemptID,
220			WorkerID:  workerID,
221			ProjectID: projectID,
222			BeadID:    beadID,
223			Harness:   harness,
224			Model:     model,
225			Profile:   profile,
226			Phase:     phase,
227			PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
228			Heartbeat: heartbeat,
229			TS:        time.Now().UTC(),
230			ElapsedMS: elapsedMS,
231		}
232	}

234	func randomProgressID() string {
235		return fmt.Sprintf("%x", time.Now().UnixNano())[:8]
236	}
237	
238	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
239		if w.Store == nil {
240			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: store is required")
241		}
242		if w.Executor == nil {
243			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: executor is required")
244		}
245	
246		now := w.Now
247		if now == nil {
248			now = time.Now
249		}
250		assignee := rcfg.Assignee()
251		if assignee == "" {
252			assignee = "ddx"
253		}
254		noProgressCooldown := rcfg.NoProgressCooldown()
255		if noProgressCooldown <= 0 {
256			noProgressCooldown = 6 * time.Hour
257		}
258		maxNoChangesBeforeClose := rcfg.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose()
259		if maxNoChangesBeforeClose <= 0 {
260			maxNoChangesBeforeClose = 3
261		}
262		heartbeatInterval := rcfg.HeartbeatInterval()
263		if heartbeatInterval <= 0 {
264			heartbeatInterval = bead.HeartbeatInterval
265		}
266		harness := rcfg.Harness()
267		model := rcfg.Model()
268		profile := rcfg.Profile()
269	
270		result := &ExecuteBeadLoopResult{}
271		attempted := make(map[string]struct{})
272	
273		emit := func(eventType string, data map[string]any) {
274			writeLoopEvent(runtime.EventSink, runtime.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())
275		}
276	
277		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{
278			"worker_id":    runtime.WorkerID,
279			"project_root": runtime.ProjectRoot,
280			"harness":      harness,
281			"model":        model,
282			"session_id":   runtime.SessionID,
283			"assignee":     assignee,
284			"once":         runtime.Once,
285		})
286		defer func() {
287			emit("loop.end", map[string]any{
288				"attempts":            result.Attempts,
289				"successes":           result.Successes,
290				"failures":            result.Failures,
291				"last_failure_status": result.LastFailureStatus,
292			})
293		}()
294	
295		for {
296			// Respect context cancellation between iterations. Without this check,
297			// a Stop() request (which cancels ctx) would only take effect during
298			// the idle poll sleep — the loop would happily claim the next ready
299			// bead as soon as the current Execute returned, ignoring the cancel.
300			if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
301				return result, err
302			}
303	
304			candidate, ok, err := w.nextCandidate(attempted, runtime.LabelFilter)
305			if err != nil {
306				return result, err
307			}
308			if !ok {
309				if result.Attempts == 0 {
310					result.NoReadyWork = true
311					if diag, ok := w.Store.(readyDiagnoser); ok {
312						if breakdown, bErr := diag.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); bErr == nil {
313							result.NoReadyWorkDetail = NoReadyWorkBreakdown{
314								SkippedEpics:       breakdown.SkippedEpics,
315								SkippedOnCooldown:  breakdown.SkippedOnCooldown,
316								SkippedNotEligible: breakdown.SkippedNotEligible,
317								SkippedSuperseded:  breakdown.SkippedSuperseded,
318								NextRetryAfter:     breakdown.NextRetryAfter,
319							}
320						}
321					}
322				}
323				if runtime.PollInterval <= 0 {
324					return result, nil
325				}
326				if err := sleepWithContext(ctx, runtime.PollInterval); err != nil {
327					return result, err
328				}
329				continue
330			}
331	
332			attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
333	
334			// Pre-claim hook: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming.
335			// On error the bead is skipped for this iteration; the loop
336			// continues (ctx is not cancelled).
337			if runtime.PreClaimHook != nil {
338				if hookErr := runtime.PreClaimHook(ctx); hookErr != nil {
339					if runtime.Log != nil {
340						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "pre-claim hook: %v (skipping %s)\n", hookErr, candidate.ID)
341					}
342					emit("preclaim.skipped", map[string]any{
343						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
344						"reason":  hookErr.Error(),
345					})
346					continue
347				}
348			}
349	
350			if err := w.Store.Claim(candidate.ID, assignee); err != nil {
351				continue
352			}
353	
354			emit("bead.claimed", map[string]any{
355				"bead_id":  candidate.ID,
356				"title":    candidate.Title,
357				"assignee": assignee,
358			})
359	
360			if runtime.Log != nil {
361				if candidate.Title != "" {
362					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "\n▶ %s: %s\n", candidate.ID, candidate.Title)
363				} else {
364					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "\n▶ %s\n", candidate.ID)
365				}
366			}
367	
368			// Generate a provisional attempt_id for progress events.
369			// The real attempt_id is assigned inside ExecuteBead; we use this
370			// for queueing/running events and replace with the real one once known.
371			provAttemptID := time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405") + "-" + randomProgressID()
372			runStart := now()
373			phaseSeq := 0
374			nextPhase := func(phase string, heartbeat bool) {
375				phaseSeq++
376				emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, newProgressEvent(
377					runtime.WorkerID, runtime.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, provAttemptID,
378					harness, model, profile,
379					phase, phaseSeq, heartbeat, now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
380				))
381			}
382	
383			nextPhase("queueing", false)
384	
385			hbCtx, hbCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
386			var hbWG sync.WaitGroup
387			hbWG.Add(1)
388			go func(beadID string) {
389				defer hbWG.Done()
390				ticker := time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval)
391				defer ticker.Stop()
392				for {
393					select {
394					case <-hbCtx.Done():
395						return
396					case <-ticker.C:
397						_ = w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID)
398					}
399				}
400			}(candidate.ID)
401	
402			nextPhase("running", false)
403	
404			report, err := w.Executor.Execute(ctx, candidate.ID)
405			hbCancel()
406			hbWG.Wait()
407			if err != nil {
408				report = ExecuteBeadReport{
409					BeadID: candidate.ID,
410					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
411					Detail: err.Error(),
412				}
413			}
414			if report.BeadID == "" {
415				report.BeadID = candidate.ID
416			}
417			if report.Status == "" {
418				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
419			}
420			if report.Detail == "" {
421				report.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(report.Status, "", "")
422			}
423	
424			result.Attempts++
425	
426			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
427				appendLoopRoutingEvidence(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, now().UTC())
428				// Close the bead early when review is skipped. The closure gate
429				// (ddx-e30e60a9) accepts this path because closing_commit_sha is
430				// set and there is no malformed-APPROVE event to reject.
431				reviewApproved := true
432				reviewSkipped := w.Reviewer == nil || runtime.NoReview || HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip")
433	
434				if reviewSkipped {
435					if err := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); err != nil {
436						return result, err
437					}
438				}
439	
440				if w.Reviewer != nil && !runtime.NoReview && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip") {
441					reviewRes, reviewErr := w.Reviewer.ReviewBead(ctx, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev, report.Harness, report.Model)
442					if reviewErr != nil {
443						// FEAT-022 §12+§14: classify the failure into the four-class
444						// taxonomy and count prior review-error events scoped to the
445						// current result_rev. On the Nth failure emit a terminal
446						// review-manual-required event instead of yet another
447						// review-error, parking the bead so a subsequent loop
448						// iteration does NOT re-execute primary.
449						class := classifyReviewError(reviewErr, reviewRes)
450						prior := countPriorReviewErrors(w.Store, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev)
451						attemptCount := prior + 1
452						maxRetries := rcfg.ReviewMaxRetries()
453						if maxRetries <= 0 {
454							maxRetries = DefaultReviewMaxRetries
455						}
456						errSummary := EventBodySummary{
457							InputBytes:  0,
458							OutputBytes: 0,
459						}
460						if reviewRes != nil {
461							errSummary = EventBodySummary{
462								Harness:     reviewRes.ReviewerHarness,
463								Model:       reviewRes.ReviewerModel,
464								InputBytes:  reviewRes.InputBytes,
465								OutputBytes: reviewRes.OutputBytes,
466								ElapsedMS:   reviewRes.DurationMS,
467							}
468						}
469						if attemptCount >= maxRetries {
470							body := appendEventSummary(reviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error()), errSummary)
471							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
472								Kind:      "review-manual-required",
473								Summary:   class,
474								Body:      body,
475								Actor:     assignee,
476								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
477								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
478							})
479							// Park the bead with a long cooldown so subsequent
480							// loop iterations do NOT re-pick it for primary
481							// re-execution. Reviewer-failure invariant (§13) is
482							// preserved: the bead is NOT closed.
483							parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(365 * 24 * time.Hour)
484							_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, "review-manual-required", class)
485						} else {
486							body := appendEventSummary(reviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error()), errSummary)
487							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
488								Kind:      "review-error",
489								Summary:   class,
490								Body:      body,
491								Actor:     assignee,
492								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
493								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
494							})
495						}
496					} else {
497						report.ReviewVerdict = string(reviewRes.Verdict)
498						report.ReviewRationale = reviewRes.Rationale
499						// Persist the full reviewer stream as an artifact so the
500						// event body never carries the raw stream (ddx-f8a11202).
501						// On error the artifact path is empty and the event body
502						// still contains the short verdict summary; callers of this
503						// loop can recover the full text from the reviewer session
504						// log if the artifact write failed.
505						artifactPath, artifactErr := persistReviewerStream(runtime.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, report.AttemptID, reviewRes.RawOutput)
506						if artifactErr != nil && runtime.Log != nil {
507							_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "reviewer stream artifact: %v\n", artifactErr)
508						}
509	
510						reviewSummary := EventBodySummary{
511							Harness:     reviewRes.ReviewerHarness,
512							Model:       reviewRes.ReviewerModel,
513							InputBytes:  reviewRes.InputBytes,
514							OutputBytes: reviewRes.OutputBytes,
515							ElapsedMS:   reviewRes.DurationMS,
516						}
517						switch reviewRes.Verdict {
518						case VerdictApprove:
519							// Approved: record the verdict event and then close.
520							// Closure must land AFTER the review event so the
521							// gate (ddx-e30e60a9) sees the terminal verdict.
522							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
523								Kind:      "review",
524								Summary:   "APPROVE",
525								Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("APPROVE", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath), reviewSummary),
526								Actor:     assignee,
527								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
528								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
529							})
530							if cerr := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); cerr != nil {
531								return result, cerr
532							}
533						case VerdictRequestChanges:
534							// Needs fixes: record the review verdict, then reopen
535							// with findings in notes. The review event must land
536							// even on non-approve paths so review-outcomes can
537							// attribute the rejection to the originating tier.
538							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
539								Kind:      "review",
540								Summary:   "REQUEST_CHANGES",
541								Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("REQUEST_CHANGES", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath), reviewSummary),
542								Actor:     assignee,
543								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
544								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
545							})
546							reopenNotes := reviewRes.Rationale
547							if reopenNotes == "" {
548								reopenNotes = reviewRes.RawOutput
549							}
550							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: REQUEST_CHANGES", reopenNotes); reopenErr != nil {
551								return result, reopenErr
552							}
553							report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges
554							report.Detail = "post-merge review: REQUEST_CHANGES"
555							reviewApproved = false
556						case VerdictBlock:
557							rationale := strings.TrimSpace(reviewRes.Rationale)
558							if rationale == "" {
559								_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
560									Kind:      "review-malfunction",
561									Summary:   "BLOCK without rationale",
562									Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("BLOCK without rationale", "", artifactPath), reviewSummary),
563									Actor:     assignee,
564									Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
565									CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
566								})
567								report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
568								report.Detail = "post-merge review: malformed BLOCK verdict (missing rationale)"
569								report.ReviewRationale = ""
570								reviewApproved = false
571								break
572							}
573							// Cannot proceed: record the verdict, then reopen and
574							// flag for human with BLOCK marker plus actionable rationale.
575							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
576								Kind:      "review",
577								Summary:   "BLOCK",
578								Body:      appendEventSummary(rationale, reviewSummary),
579								Actor:     assignee,
580								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
581								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
582							})
583							blockNotes := "REVIEW:BLOCK\n\n" + rationale
584							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: BLOCK", blockNotes); reopenErr != nil {
585								return result, reopenErr
586							}
587							report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock
588							report.Detail = "post-merge review: BLOCK (flagged for human)"
589							reviewApproved = false
590						}
591					}
592				}
593	
594				if reviewApproved {
595					result.Successes++
596					result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
597				} else {
598					result.Failures++
599					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
600				}
601			} else {
602				if err := w.Store.Unclaim(candidate.ID); err != nil {
603					return result, err
604				}
605				if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges {
606					count, cerr := w.Store.IncrNoChangesCount(candidate.ID)
607					if cerr != nil {
608						return result, cerr
609					}
610					satisfied, evidence, aerr := w.adjudicateNoChanges(ctx, candidate.ID, count, maxNoChangesBeforeClose, report.NoChangesRationale, candidate.Acceptance, runtime.ProjectRoot)
611					if aerr != nil {
612						return result, aerr
613					}
614					if satisfied {
615						// Adjudication confirmed bead is already satisfied.
616						// Set the terminal status BEFORE the close so the late
617						// executeBeadLoopEvent append captures "already_satisfied"
618						// (not "no_changes"), and emit an early execute-bead
619						// evidence event so the closure gate accepts even when
620						// BaseRev is empty (test fixtures and genuinely-no-commit
621						// satisfied beads).
622						report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied
623						if evidence != "" {
624							// Checker evidence explains why the bead is being closed;
625							// it takes precedence over the executor's attempt detail.
626							report.Detail = evidence
627						}
628						_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC()))
629						if cerr := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.BaseRev); cerr != nil {
630							return result, cerr
631						}
632						result.Successes++
633						result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
634					} else {
635						// Unresolved: suppress immediate retry so the queue can
636						// move on to other beads.
637						if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
638							retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(noProgressCooldown)
639							if cerr := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail); cerr != nil {
640								return result, cerr
641							}
642							report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
643						}
644						result.Failures++
645						result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
646					}
647				} else if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition {
648					// Executor declined the bead because it is too large to
649					// deliver in one pass. Record a structured
650					// `decomposition-recommendation` event and park the bead
651					// long enough that subsequent loop iterations do NOT
652					// re-attempt it. An operator (or helix-evolve) clears the
653					// cooldown — typically by splitting the bead.
654					body, mErr := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
655						"rationale":            report.DecompositionRationale,
656						"recommended_subbeads": report.DecompositionRecommendation,
657						"detail":               report.Detail,
658						"base_rev":             report.BaseRev,
659						"session_id":           report.SessionID,
660					})
661					bodyStr := ""
662					if mErr == nil {
663						bodyStr = string(body)
664					} else {
665						bodyStr = fmt.Sprintf("rationale=%s\nrecommended_subbeads=%v",
666							report.DecompositionRationale, report.DecompositionRecommendation)
667					}
668					summary := "agent declined: needs decomposition"
669					if n := len(report.DecompositionRecommendation); n > 0 {
670						summary = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%d sub-beads)", summary, n)
671					}
672					_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
673						Kind:      "decomposition-recommendation",
674						Summary:   summary,
675						Body:      bodyStr,
676						Actor:     assignee,
677						Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
678						CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
679					})
680					parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(365 * 24 * time.Hour)
681					if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
682						return result, err
683					}
684					report.RetryAfter = parkUntil.Format(time.RFC3339)
685					result.Failures++
686					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
687				} else if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed {
688					// Push failed after a successful local merge/ff. The bead is
689					// NOT closed (commits live only locally) — park it long enough
690					// that subsequent loop iterations refuse to re-pick it until
691					// an operator clears the state. The push stderr in
692					// report.Detail is recorded as last_detail so it surfaces on
693					// the bead and on any direct claim attempt.
694					parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(365 * 24 * time.Hour)
695					if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
696						return result, err
697					}
698					report.RetryAfter = parkUntil.Format(time.RFC3339)
699					result.Failures++
700					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
701				} else {
702					if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
703						retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(noProgressCooldown)
704						if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
705							return result, err
706						}
707						report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
708					}
709					result.Failures++
710					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
711				}
712			}
713	
714			result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
715	
716			// Skip the late execute-bead append for already-satisfied beads —
717			// the satisfied path appends its own terminal event before
718			// CloseWithEvidence so the closure gate sees execution evidence.
719			// Duplicating it here would yield two identical events.
720			if report.Status != ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
721				if err := w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC())); err != nil {
722					return result, err
723				}
724			}
725	
726			// Emit terminal progress phase event.
727			terminalPhase := "failed"
728			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess || report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
729				terminalPhase = "done"
730			} else if report.PreserveRef != "" {
731				terminalPhase = "preserved"
732			}
733			// Use the real attempt_id from the report if available.
734			finalAttemptID := report.AttemptID
735			if finalAttemptID == "" {
736				finalAttemptID = provAttemptID
737			}
738			phaseSeq++
739			emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, ProgressEvent{
740				EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
741				AttemptID: finalAttemptID,
742				WorkerID:  runtime.WorkerID,
743				ProjectID: runtime.ProjectRoot,
744				BeadID:    candidate.ID,
745				Harness:   harness,
746				Model:     model,
747				Profile:   profile,
748				Phase:     terminalPhase,
749				PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
750				Heartbeat: false,
751				TS:        now().UTC(),
752				ElapsedMS: now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
753				Message:   report.Detail,
754			})
755	
756			emit("bead.result", map[string]any{
757				"bead_id":              candidate.ID,
758				"status":               report.Status,
759				"detail":               report.Detail,
760				"session_id":           report.SessionID,
761				"result_rev":           report.ResultRev,
762				"base_rev":             report.BaseRev,
763				"preserve_ref":         report.PreserveRef,
764				"no_changes_rationale": report.NoChangesRationale,
765				"duration_ms":          now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
766			})
767	
768			if runtime.Log != nil {
769				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "✓ %s → %s\n", candidate.ID, formatLoopResult(report))
770			}
771	
772			if runtime.Once {
773				return result, nil
774			}
775		}
776	}

778	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) nextCandidate(attempted map[string]struct{}, labelFilter string) (bead.Bead, bool, error) {
779		ready, err := w.Store.ReadyExecution()
780		if err != nil {
781			return bead.Bead{}, false, err
782		}
783		for _, candidate := range ready {
784			if _, seen := attempted[candidate.ID]; seen {
785				continue
786			}
787			if labelFilter != "" && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, labelFilter) {
788				continue
789			}
790			return candidate, true, nil
791		}
792		return bead.Bead{}, false, nil
793	}
794	
795	// appendLoopRoutingEvidence records a kind:routing evidence event on the bead
796	// from the executor's ExecuteBeadReport, so that review-outcomes analytics can
797	// attribute a subsequent review verdict to the originating provider/model tier.
798	// Best-effort: errors and missing-provider cases are silently ignored.
799	func appendLoopRoutingEvidence(store BeadEventAppender, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport, createdAt time.Time) {
800		if store == nil || beadID == "" {
801			return
802		}
803		provider := report.Provider
804		if provider == "" {
805			provider = report.Harness
806		}
807		if provider == "" {
808			return
809		}
810		body, err := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
811			"resolved_provider": provider,
812			"resolved_model":    report.Model,
813			"fallback_chain":    []string{},
814			"requested_profile": report.RequestedProfile,
815			"requested_tier":    report.RequestedTier,
816			"resolved_tier":     report.ResolvedTier,
817			"escalation_count":  report.EscalationCount,
818			"final_tier":        report.FinalTier,
819		})
820		if err != nil {
821			return
822		}
823		summary := "provider=" + provider
824		if report.Model != "" {
825			summary += " model=" + report.Model
826		}
827		_ = store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
828			Kind:      "routing",
829			Summary:   summary,
830			Body:      string(body),
831			Actor:     "ddx",
832			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
833			CreatedAt: createdAt,
834		})
835	}

877	// writeLoopEvent emits one structured JSONL line to sink describing a
878	// milestone in an execute-bead loop run. Entries use the same envelope as
879	// the ddx-agent harness (session_id/seq/type/ts/data) so existing log
880	// aggregators (FormatSessionLogLines, ddx server workers log) can parse
881	// the stream uniformly. Errors are swallowed: structured logging must
882	// never break the core execute-loop.
883	func writeLoopEvent(sink io.Writer, sessionID, eventType string, data map[string]any, ts time.Time) {
884		if sink == nil {
885			return
886		}
887		if data == nil {
888			data = map[string]any{}
889		}
890		entry := map[string]any{
891			"session_id": sessionID,
892			"type":       eventType,
893			"ts":         ts.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
894			"data":       data,
895		}
896		line, err := json.Marshal(entry)
897		if err != nil {
898			return
899		}
900		_, _ = sink.Write(line)
901		_, _ = sink.Write([]byte("\n"))
902	}

904	// formatLoopResult returns a concise human-readable summary of a bead execution
905	// result using merged/preserved/error terminology instead of raw status codes.
906	func formatLoopResult(report ExecuteBeadReport) string {
907		switch report.Status {
908		case ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess:
909			shortRev := report.ResultRev
910			if len(shortRev) > 8 {
911				shortRev = shortRev[:8]
912			}
913			if shortRev != "" {
914				return fmt.Sprintf("merged (%s)", shortRev)
915			}
916			return "merged"
917		case ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
918			return "already_satisfied"
919		case ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges:
920			if report.NoChangesRationale != "" {
921				return fmt.Sprintf("no_changes: %s", report.NoChangesRationale)
922			}
923			return "no_changes"
924		default:
925			detail := report.Detail
926			if detail == "" {
927				detail = report.Status
928			}
929			if report.PreserveRef != "" {
930				return fmt.Sprintf("preserved: %s", detail)
931			}
932			return fmt.Sprintf("error: %s", detail)
933		}
934	}

936	// reCommitSHA matches a 7-to-40 character lowercase hex string that looks like a
937	// git commit SHA. Used to detect whether a no_changes rationale cites a prior commit.
938	var reCommitSHA = regexp.MustCompile(`\b[0-9a-f]{7,40}\b`)
939	
940	// reTestFuncName matches a Go test function name (TestXxx or BenchmarkXxx).
941	var reTestFuncName = regexp.MustCompile(`\b(?:Test|Benchmark)[A-Z]\w*\b`)
942	
943	// rationaleIsSpecific returns true when the rationale string contains a reference
944	// specific enough to treat a no_changes outcome as already_satisfied on the first
945	// attempt. Currently this means: the rationale cites a commit SHA (7+ hex chars)
946	// or a Go test function name. Vague rationales ("nothing to do") return false.
947	func rationaleIsSpecific(rationale string) bool {
948		if rationale == "" {
949			return false
950		}
951		return reCommitSHA.MatchString(rationale) || reTestFuncName.MatchString(rationale)
952	}

954	// adjudicateNoChanges runs the no-change adjudication step for a bead.
955	// It returns (satisfied, evidence, err). When satisfied is true the bead
956	// should be closed as already_satisfied with the evidence string. When false
957	// retry suppression (cooldown) should be applied and the bead left open.
958	//
959	// If a SatisfactionChecker is configured it is called first. Otherwise:
960	//   - When the report carries a specific rationale (cites a commit SHA or test
961	//     name), the bead is closed as already_satisfied on the first occurrence.
962	//   - Otherwise the default count-based rule applies (close after maxNoChangesBeforeClose).
963	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) adjudicateNoChanges(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount, maxNoChangesBeforeClose int, rationale, acceptance, projectRoot string) (bool, string, error) {
964		if w.SatisfactionChecker != nil {
965			return w.SatisfactionChecker.CheckSatisfied(ctx, beadID, noChangesCount)
966		}
967		candidate := false
968		evidence := ""
969		switch {
970		case rationaleIsSpecific(rationale):
971			candidate = true
972			evidence = rationale
973		case noChangesCount >= maxNoChangesBeforeClose:
974			candidate = true
975			evidence = fmt.Sprintf("no_changes on %d consecutive attempt(s); bead treated as already satisfied", noChangesCount)
976		}
977		if !candidate {
978			return false, "", nil
979		}
980		// Tighten the gate: when AC names structural properties (test functions,
981		// deleted files, removed struct fields), refuse already_satisfied unless
982		// each property holds in the worktree / rationale. Prevents false closes
983		// where a regression suite passes but the AC's specific contract is unmet.
984		if claims := ParseACClaims(acceptance); len(claims) > 0 {
985			if ok, why := VerifyACClaims(claims, projectRoot, rationale); !ok {
986				return false, why, nil
987			}
988		}
989		return true, evidence, nil
990	}

999	// classifyReviewError maps a reviewer error and partial result into one of the
1000	// four FEAT-022 §12 taxonomy classes. Resolution order:
1001	//  1. reviewRes.Error if it's already one of the canonical class identifiers
1002	//     (the reviewer set it explicitly — preferred path).
1003	//  2. The error string text (for backwards-compatible reviewers that only
1004	//     embed the class in their message).
1005	//  3. ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable as a fallback when the strict-parse error
1006	//     leaks through a reviewer that did not set Error.
1007	//  4. Default to transport (network/provider-side failure) when nothing else
1008	//     matches.
1009	func classifyReviewError(reviewErr error, reviewRes *ReviewResult) string {
1010		if reviewRes != nil {
1011			switch reviewRes.Error {
1012			case evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
1013				evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
1014				evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable,
1015				evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport:
1016				return reviewRes.Error
1017			}
1018		}
1019		msg := ""
1020		if reviewErr != nil {
1021			msg = reviewErr.Error()
1022		}
1023		switch {
1024		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow):
1025			return evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow
1026		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty):
1027			return evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty
1028		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable):
1029			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
1030		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport):
1031			return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
1032		case reviewErr != nil && strings.Contains(msg, ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable.Error()):
1033			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
1034		}
1035		return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
1036	}

1038	// reResultRevField extracts the result_rev value from a structured review-error
1039	// event body (one `result_rev=<sha>` line per event, written by
1040	// reviewErrorEventBody). The format is intentionally line-oriented so it
1041	// survives the AppendEvent body cap without losing the rev association.
1042	var reResultRevField = regexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^result_rev=([^\s]+)\s*$`)
1043	
1044	// countPriorReviewErrors returns the number of `review-error` events already
1045	// recorded against this bead whose body cites the given result_rev. This is
1046	// the FEAT-022 §14 retry counter — it is event-scoped (no separate counter
1047	// field on the bead) so a fresh result_rev naturally resets the count.
1048	func countPriorReviewErrors(store ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID, resultRev string) int {
1049		if resultRev == "" {
1050			return 0
1051		}
1052		events, err := store.Events(beadID)
1053		if err != nil {
1054			return 0
1055		}
1056		n := 0
1057		for _, ev := range events {
1058			if ev.Kind != "review-error" {
1059				continue
1060			}
1061			m := reResultRevField.FindStringSubmatch(ev.Body)
1062			if m == nil {
1063				continue
1064			}
1065			if m[1] == resultRev {
1066				n++
1067			}
1068		}
1069		return n
1070	}
1071	
1072	// reviewErrorEventBody is the canonical body shape for review-error and
1073	// review-manual-required events. It carries the failure class, attempt count,
1074	// and result_rev as discrete lines so operators can grep without parsing the
1075	// full reviewer error text. The trailing free-form message is the raw
1076	// reviewer-error string for forensics.
1077	func reviewErrorEventBody(class string, attemptCount int, resultRev, message string) string {
1078		var b strings.Builder
1079		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "failure_class=%s\n", class)
1080		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "attempt_count=%d\n", attemptCount)
1081		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "result_rev=%s\n", resultRev)
1082		if message != "" {
1083			b.WriteString("\n")
1084			b.WriteString(message)
1085		}
1086		return b.String()
1087	}

1089	func sleepWithContext(ctx context.Context, d time.Duration) error {
1090		if d <= 0 {
1091			return nil
1092		}
1093		timer := time.NewTimer(d)
1094		defer timer.Stop()
1095		select {
1096		case <-ctx.Done():
1097			return ctx.Err()
1098		case <-timer.C:
1099			return nil
1100		}
1101	}

1512	func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentExecuteLoopCommand() *cobra.Command {
1513		cmd := &cobra.Command{
1514			Use:   "execute-loop",
1515			Short: "Drain the single-project execution-ready bead queue",
1516			Long: `execute-loop is the primary queue-driven execution surface. It scans the
1517	target project's execution-ready bead queue, claims the next ready bead,
1518	runs "ddx agent execute-bead" on it from the project root, records the
1519	structured result, and continues until no unattempted ready work remains.
1520	
1521	Reach for execute-loop by default. Use "ddx agent execute-bead" directly
1522	only as the primitive for debugging or re-running one specific bead.
1523	
1524	Planning and document-only beads are valid execution targets — any bead
1525	with unmet acceptance criteria and no blocking deps is eligible.
1526	
1527	Close semantics (per execute-bead result status):
1528	  success                      — close bead with session + commit evidence
1529	  already_satisfied            — close bead (after repeated no_changes)
1530	  no_changes                   — unclaim; may cooldown or close after retries
1531	  land_conflict                — unclaim; result preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/
1532	  post_run_check_failed        — unclaim; result preserved
1533	  execution_failed             — unclaim
1534	  structural_validation_failed — unclaim
1535	
1536	Only success (and already_satisfied) closes the bead. Every other status
1537	leaves the bead open and unclaimed so a later attempt can try again. Each
1538	attempt is appended to the bead as an execute-bead event (status, detail,
1539	base_rev, result_rev, preserve_ref, retry_after), and the underlying agent
1540	session log is recorded under the execute-bead agent-log path.
1541	
1542	By default execute-loop submits to the running ddx server as a background
1543	worker and returns immediately. Use --local to run inline in the current
1544	process.
1545	
1546	Project targeting (multi-project servers):
1547	  --project <path>    target a specific project root (absolute path or name)
1548	  DDX_PROJECT_ROOT    env var fallback; used when --project is not set
1549	  (default)           the git root of the current working directory
1550	
1551	When submitting to a multi-project server you must ensure the target project
1552	is registered with the server (run "ddx server" from that directory, or use
1553	"ddx server projects register"). The server rejects unrecognised project paths.
1554	`,
1555			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue once and exit (normal surface)
1556	  ddx agent execute-loop
1557	
1558	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
1559	  ddx agent execute-loop --profile default --once
1560	
1561	  # Run continuously as a bounded queue worker
1562	  ddx agent execute-loop --poll-interval 30s
1563	
1564	  # Force a specific harness/model for a debugging pass
1565	  ddx agent execute-loop --once --harness codex
1566	  ddx agent execute-loop --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7

39	func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentExecuteBeadCommand() *cobra.Command {
40		cmd := &cobra.Command{
41			Use:   "execute-bead <bead-id>",
42			Short: "Run an agent on one bead in an isolated worktree, then land the result",
43			Long: `execute-bead is the primitive: it runs a single agent on a single bead in
44	an isolated git worktree (the worker step), then hands the result to the
45	parent-side orchestrator which merges or preserves the commits.
46	
47	The worker classifies its outcome as one of:
48	  task_succeeded  — agent exited 0 and produced commits
49	  task_no_changes — agent exited 0 but made no commits
50	  task_failed     — agent exited non-zero
51	
52	The orchestrator then applies landing logic (merge, gate evaluation, preserve)
53	and the final result reflects both the worker and landing decisions.
54	
55	For normal queue-driven work use "ddx agent execute-loop", which claims
56	ready beads, calls this command, and closes or unclaims each bead based on
57	the result. Reach for execute-bead directly only to debug or re-run a
58	specific bead.
59	
60	Result status is reported on the "status:" line and is one of:
61	  success                          — merged (or preserved with --no-merge)
62	  no_changes                       — agent ran but produced no diff
63	  already_satisfied                — closed by the loop after repeated no_changes
64	  land_conflict                    — merge failed; result preserved
65	  post_run_check_failed            — checks failed; result preserved
66	  execution_failed                 — agent or harness error
67	  structural_validation_failed     — bead or prompt inputs invalid
68	
69	execute-loop closes the bead with session/commit evidence only on success
70	(and already_satisfied); every other status leaves the bead open and
71	unclaimed for a later attempt.`,
72			Example: `  # Debug one specific bead (prefer "execute-loop" for normal queue work)
73	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c
74	
75	  # Run against a non-HEAD base revision
76	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --from main
77	
78	  # Preserve the result under refs/ddx/iterations/... instead of merging
79	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --no-merge
80	
81	  # Pin harness/model for a debugging pass
82	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --harness codex`,
83			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
84			RunE: f.runAgentExecuteBead,
85		}
86		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Project root path (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
87		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
88		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-merge", false, "Skip merge; preserve result under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<timestamp>-<base-shortsha> instead")
89		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness to use")
90		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model override")
91		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider name (e.g. vidar, openrouter); selects a named provider from config")
92		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model catalog reference (e.g. code-medium); resolved via the model catalog")
93		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
94		cmd.Flags().String("context-budget", "", "Context budget for prompt: empty (full), minimal (omit large governing docs for cheap-tier)")
95		cmd.Flags().String("prompt", "", "Prompt file path (auto-generated from bead if omitted)")
96		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output result as JSON")
97		return cmd

100	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecuteBead(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
101		beadID := args[0]
102		if !validBeadID.MatchString(beadID) {
103			return fmt.Errorf("invalid bead ID %q: must contain only letters, digits, dots, underscores, and hyphens", beadID)
104		}
105		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
106		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
107		noMerge, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-merge")
108		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
109		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
110		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
111		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
112		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
113		contextBudget, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("context-budget")
114		promptFile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("prompt")
115		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
116	
117		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
118	
119		rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, config.CLIOverrides{
120			Harness:       harness,
121			Model:         model,
122			Provider:      provider,
123			ModelRef:      modelRef,
124			Effort:        effort,
125			ContextBudget: contextBudget,
126		})
127		if err != nil {
128			return fmt.Errorf("load config at %s: %w", projectRoot, err)
129		}
130		runtime := agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime{
131			FromRev:    fromRev,
132			PromptFile: promptFile,
133			WorkerID:   os.Getenv("DDX_WORKER_ID"),
134			BeadEvents: bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
135		}
136	
137		var gitOps agent.GitOps = &agent.RealGitOps{}
138		if f.executeBeadGitOverride != nil {
139			gitOps = f.executeBeadGitOverride
140		}
141	
142		var orchestratorGitOps agent.OrchestratorGitOps = &agent.RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
143		if f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride != nil {
144			orchestratorGitOps = f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride
145		}
146	
147		// Test injection seam: AgentRunnerOverride feeds canned *Result values
148		// from fakes without spinning up a real provider. Production callers
149		// leave this nil so ExecuteBead constructs an agent service from
150		// projectRoot internally.
151		if f.AgentRunnerOverride != nil {
152			runtime.AgentRunner = f.AgentRunnerOverride
153		}
154	
155		// Preflight the orphan-model check before creating a worktree. Mirrors
156		// the execute-loop preflight so `ddx agent execute-bead --model <unroutable>`
157		// errors before any git work happens rather than mid-execution. Skip the
158		// preflight when a test runner override is in use — overrides may be
159		// fakes that bypass routing entirely.
160		if f.AgentRunnerOverride == nil {
161			if err := agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(cmd.Context(), f.WorkingDir, harness, model, provider, modelRef); err != nil {
162				return err
163			}
164		}
165	
166		// Recover any orphaned worktrees from previous crashed runs before
167		// spawning a new worker. This is the parent's responsibility.
168		agent.RecoverOrphans(gitOps, projectRoot, beadID)
169	
170		// Worker step: run the agent in an isolated worktree.
171		res, err := agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig(cmd.Context(), projectRoot, beadID, rcfg, runtime, gitOps)
172		if err != nil && res == nil {
173			return err
174		}
175	
176		// Orchestrator step: land the result (ff or merge → push, gate eval,
177		// preserve). Always run when we have a result — the orchestrator handles
178		// all cases including no-changes and error (agent failed with no commits).
179		//
180		// Single-bead CLI is strictly serial (no sibling workers in this
181		// process), so we can wire the LandingAdvancer to call Land() directly
182		// without going through a per-project coordinator goroutine. Tests may
183		// inject a custom advancer via f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride.
184		if res != nil {
185			var advancer func(r *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error)
186			if f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride != nil {
187				advancer = f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride
188			} else {
189				landingGitOps := landingGitOpsFromFactory(f)
190				advancer = func(r *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error) {
191					return agent.Land(projectRoot, agent.BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r), landingGitOps)
192				}
193			}
194			landingOpts := agent.BeadLandingOptions{
195				NoMerge:         noMerge,
196				LandingAdvancer: advancer,
197			}
198	
199			// Wire required execution gates: when the worker manifest declares
200			// governing IDs, gate-eval them in an ephemeral worktree at ResultRev
201			// before LandBeadResult decides merge vs preserve. The original
202			// worker worktree was cleaned up by ExecuteBead, so we pin ResultRev
203			// to a transient ref and check it out into a temp worktree for the
204			// duration of gate evaluation.
205			if res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 {
206				wt, ids, cleanup, ctxErr := agent.BuildLandingGateContext(projectRoot, res, gitOps)
207				if ctxErr != nil {
208					// Soft-fail: log and skip gate eval rather than abort the land.
209					fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "ddx: warning: gate-context setup failed: %v (skipping required-gate eval)\n", ctxErr)
210				} else if wt != "" {
211					defer cleanup()
212					landingOpts.WtPath = wt
213					landingOpts.GovernIDs = ids
214					landingOpts.ChecksArtifactPath = filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir, "checks.json")
215					landingOpts.ChecksArtifactRel = filepath.Join(res.ExecutionDir, "checks.json")
216				}
217			}
218	
219			if landing, landErr := agent.LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchestratorGitOps, landingOpts); landErr == nil {
220				agent.ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
221			} else if err == nil {
222				err = landErr
223			}
224		}
225	
226		if err != nil {
227			_ = writeExecuteBeadResult(cmd, res, asJSON)
228			return err
229		}
230	
231		return writeExecuteBeadResult(cmd, res, asJSON)
